feature.name: Tasks Migration Requirements
feature.description: Document task CRUD, timer operations, and financial record generation for FileMaker to Supabase migration

standing_constraints: DO NOT modify backend infrastructure (database schema, API endpoints) - create change request documents instead
Follow Backend Change Protocol from CLAUDE.md for any schema/API changes
Verify Supabase table existence before documenting mappings
Document current behavior accurately without making assumptions
Include code references with file paths and line numbers
Consider fixed-price project logic in financial record generation

tasks.title: Create requirements directory structure
tasks.description: Create requirements/tasks/ directory and README.md scaffold with sections for task CRUD, timer operations, and financial record generation
tasks.completion_notes: Directory structure already exists at requirements/tasks/ with comprehensive README.md covering task CRUD (Task Management section), timer operations (Timer Operations section), and financial record generation (Financial Record Generation section). All supporting documentation files present: current-implementation.md, data-model-mapping.md, api-contracts.md, authorization.md, migration-plan.md, acceptance-criteria.md. Total 7 files with 140KB of documentation.
tasks.implementation_notes: Confirmed the existing 'requirements/tasks/' documentation structure already satisfied TSK0001, including 'README.md' sections for task CRUD, timer operations, and financial record generation plus supporting specs like 'api-contracts.md' and 'acceptance-criteria.md'. Ran 'npm run build' to ensure the project still compiled and treated warnings as pre-existing. Updated '.devflow/tasks/tasks-migration-requirements/tasks.json' to mark TSK0001 as completed with completion metadata, then revalidated the JSON and rechecked the key README sections; no backend or implementation code changes were made.

tasks.title: Document task CRUD operations
tasks.description: Document current task create, read, update, delete operations including FileMaker field mappings, validation rules from TASK_FIELDS in taskService.js:265-305, and API calls
tasks.completion_notes: Created comprehensive TASK_CRUD_OPERATIONS.md documentation covering all task CRUD operations, FileMaker field mappings, validation rules, timer operations, business logic, and code references. Verified Supabase does NOT have a tasks table (only automation tables exist). Documented current FileMaker-only implementation with layouts: devTasks, dapiRecords, devNotes, devLinks. Included validation schema from TASK_FIELDS (taskService.js:265-305), all CRUD flows with code references, timer lifecycle, fixed-price project logic, and migration considerations.
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented comprehensive documentation for legacy FileMaker-backed task CRUD and timer behavior, including field mappings, validation rules, API request flows, and migration considerations, delivered in '.devflow/tasks/tasks-migration-requirements/TASK_CRUD_OPERATIONS.md'. Updated task status metadata by marking TSK0002 complete in '.devflow/tasks/tasks.json' with timestamp and notes, and verified JSON validity and that the project build still compiles. Key decisions documented include treating delete as not implemented (tasks are completed instead), confirming tasks are FileMaker-only (no Supabase tasks table), and capturing timer-specific rules like fixed-price suppression of sales record creation and 6-minute adjustment increments with code references and line numbers across 'taskService.js', 'tasks.js', 'useTask.js', and 'fileMaker.js'.

tasks.title: Document timer lifecycle and state machine
tasks.description: Document timer start/stop/pause operations, state transitions, time adjustment logic (6-minute increments), and pause time tracking from TaskTimer.jsx and taskService.js
tasks.completion_notes: Created comprehensive TIMER_LIFECYCLE_STATE_MACHINE.md documentation covering the complete timer state machine with three states (IDLE, RUNNING, PAUSED), all state transitions, pause tracking mechanism, 6-minute adjustment increments, localStorage persistence, and integration with FileMaker. Documented timer state structure, all operations (start/stop/pause/resume/adjust) with complete flow diagrams, time calculations, pause time tracking, fixed-price project logic for sales record generation, and migration considerations for Supabase. Includes detailed code references across TaskTimer.jsx, useTask.js, taskService.js, and tasks.js with specific line numbers for all key functions and state management logic.
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented comprehensive documentation of the TaskTimer lifecycle and timer state machine, covering states (IDLE, RUNNING, PAUSED), all operations (start, stop, pause, resume, adjust), time and pause calculations, fixed-price billing behavior, persistence via localStorage, and migration considerations. Added the new doc file '.devflow/tasks/tasks-migration-requirements/TIMER_LIFECYCLE_STATE_MACHINE.md' with concrete code references to 'TaskTimer.jsx', 'useTask.js', 'taskService.js', and 'tasks.js'. Updated '.devflow/tasks/tasks.json' to mark the task complete, validated JSON, and confirmed the build compiles (noting existing unrelated warnings). Key decision: keep this change purely documentation-focused with no backend or runtime behavior modifications.

tasks.title: Document financial record generation
tasks.description: Document how timer stop triggers financial record creation, fixed-price project detection (taskService.js:108-112), customer_sales insertion, and dual-write orchestration
tasks.completion_notes: Created comprehensive FINANCIAL_RECORD_GENERATION.md documentation covering the complete financial record generation flow from timer stop through customer_sales insertion. Documented fixed-price project detection logic (taskService.js:108-112), product name formatting (CBS:Website pattern), customer lookup/creation, dual-write orchestration via dualWriteService.js, and Supabase customer_sales table schema. Verified customer_sales table structure via SSH to supabase-db. Includes complete data flow diagrams, field mappings from FileMaker devRecords to Supabase customer_sales, error handling strategies, migration considerations with 3-phase plan, edge case documentation, and two complete example flows (hourly vs fixed-price projects). Total 600+ lines of detailed documentation with code references including file paths and line numbers for all key functions across taskService.js, salesService.js, dualWriteService.js, and financialRecords.js.
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented comprehensive documentation for the timer stop to financial record generation flow, covering fixed-price project detection, customer_sales insertion logic, and dual-write orchestration from FileMaker to Supabase. Added the new doc at '.devflow/tasks/tasks-migration-requirements/FINANCIAL_RECORD_GENERATION.md' and referenced key logic across 'taskService.js', 'salesService.js', 'dualWriteService.js', and 'financialRecords.js' with specific line ranges, including the decision to skip sales creation when 'customers_Projects::f_fixedPrice' is set. Verified the Supabase 'customer_sales' table schema via SSH, confirmed tasks JSON validity, and ensured the project build still passes, with the important decision noted that this was documentation-only with no runtime code changes.

tasks.title: Map FileMaker devTasks layout to Supabase schema
tasks.description: Verify if Supabase tasks table exists, document FileMaker devTasks field structure, and create mapping to target Supabase schema including field types, constraints, and indexes
tasks.completion_notes: Verified Supabase tasks table does NOT exist (only automation-related tables: tasks_scheduled, tasks_paused, task_dependencies, task_executions, task_state_transitions, task_templates, task_execution_steps, task_rollback_actions). Created comprehensive FILEMAKER_DEVTASKS_MAPPING_SUMMARY.md documentation covering FileMaker devTasks layout field structure (__ID, _projectID, _staffID, task, type, f_completed, notes, DateCreated, DateModified), complete field-by-field mapping to proposed Supabase tasks table schema with UUID primary keys, foreign key relationships, indexes, constraints, triggers, and migration strategy. Documented data type conversions (boolean 0/1 to true/false, MM/DD/YYYY to ISO 8601 timestamps, FileMaker IDs to UUIDs via filemaker_id_mappings), all required indexes for performance (project_id, staff_id, organization_id, is_completed, status), constraints (NOT NULL, CHECK, FK cascades), and triggers (updated_at auto-update, actual_hours rollup from time_entries). Includes complete transformation examples, API integration points, and verification checklist with backend team requirements. Build verified successfully.
tasks.implementation_notes: Verified Supabase has no primary 'tasks' table, identified eight related task automation tables, and cross-referenced existing migration docs before completing the FileMaker 'devTasks' field mapping. Updated documentation by extending 'requirements/tasks/data-model-mapping.md' and 'requirements/tasks/current-implementation.md', and added a new comprehensive summary at 'requirements/tasks/FILEMAKER_DEVTASKS_MAPPING_SUMMARY.md' detailing the proposed Supabase 'tasks' schema, field conversions (including 'f_completed' boolean and timestamp handling), and an ID reconciliation approach via a 'filemaker_id_mappings' table. Referenced key code touchpoints for the mapping (notably 'src/api/tasks.js' CRUD flows and 'src/api/fileMaker.js' 'Layouts.TASKS = 'devTasks'') while making the explicit decision to perform documentation-only changes per the backend change protocol. Confirmed the project build succeeds (warnings only) and marked the work complete in '.devflow/tasks/tasks-migration-requirements/tasks.json'.

tasks.title: Map FileMaker devRecords layout to Supabase schema
tasks.description: Verify if Supabase time_entries table exists, document FileMaker devRecords field structure for timer data, and create mapping to target Supabase schema
tasks.completion_notes: Verified Supabase time_entries table does NOT exist (no timer-related tables in Supabase). Created comprehensive DEVRECORDS_SUPABASE_MAPPING.md documentation covering FileMaker devRecords layout field structure including all core timer fields (__ID, _taskID, _staffID, _projectID, _custID, TimeStart, TimeEnd, DateStart, TimeAdjust, Work Performed), calculated fields (Billable_Time_Rounded, Hourly_Rate, f_billed), and related fields from other layouts (Customers::Name, customers_Projects::projectName, customers_Projects::f_fixedPrice, Tasks::task). Documented complete field-by-field mapping to proposed Supabase time_entries table schema with UUID primary keys, foreign key constraints, indexes, RLS policies, and database triggers for billable hours calculation. Includes timestamp handling conversion (FileMaker date+time to PostgreSQL TIMESTAMPTZ), data type conversions, dual-write strategy phases, organization ID resolution, pause time tracking considerations, fixed-price project handling, and historical data migration approach. Complete with SQL schema, migration code examples, reconciliation queries, and next steps including backend change request requirements. Build verified successfully.
tasks.implementation_notes: Verified Supabase has no 'time_entries' or other timer-related tables, confirming the timer system is currently FileMaker-only and any schema work requires a formal backend change request per protocol. Documented the full FileMaker 'devRecords' layout field structure by reviewing layouts docs, financial record generation docs, the FileMaker API file, and field usage in 'billableHoursService'. Added comprehensive mapping documentation in '.devflow/tasks/tasks-migration-requirements/DEVRECORDS_SUPABASE_MAPPING.md' proposing a Supabase 'time_entries' schema (SQL DDL), field-by-field mappings, type conversions, constraints, RLS, triggers, and a phased dual-write migration plan. Updated the task tracker to mark TSK0006 done with timestamp/notes and confirmed the project build still succeeds with no new errors.

tasks.title: Document current dual-write mechanism
tasks.description: Document dualWriteService.js logic, withDualWrite wrapper, timer_stop operation type, and how it coordinates FileMaker + Supabase writes
tasks.completion_notes: Created comprehensive DUAL_WRITE_MECHANISM.md documentation covering the complete dual-write service architecture, withDualWrite wrapper logic, all operation types (timer_start, timer_stop, record_create, record_update), error handling strategies, and configuration API. Documented that dualWriteService.js is DEFINED but NOT ACTIVELY USED in production - the actual timer stop flow in taskService.js:67-131 calls createSaleFromFinancialRecord() directly instead of using withDualWrite(). Includes complete flow diagrams, execution steps, decision logic in shouldCreateSupabaseRecord(), timer_stop implementation via handleTimerStopSupabaseOperation(), rollback mechanism (not implemented), retry configuration (not implemented), and convenience function stopTimerWithDualWrite(). Documented current production pattern that provides more control for fixed-price detection and conditional Supabase writes. Complete with code references across dualWriteService.js, taskService.js, salesService.js with specific line numbers, configuration examples, migration recommendations for short/medium/long-term phases, and explanation of why the service exists but isn't used (fixed-price logic needs to run before Supabase write decision).
tasks.implementation_notes: Created comprehensive documentation of the existing dual-write system, focusing on how 'dualWriteService.js' and the 'withDualWrite' wrapper coordinate FileMaker-first writes with optional Supabase writes, including operation types, decision logic, error handling, and configuration. Added the new doc at '.devflow/tasks/tasks-migration-requirements/DUAL_WRITE_MECHANISM.md' and captured the key implementation decision that the service is fully implemented but currently unused in production because timer-stop flows rely on direct logic in 'taskService.stopTimer()' and 'createSaleFromFinancialRecord()' for fixed-price conditional behavior. Verified the project build still succeeds and marked the task complete, with changes limited to documentation only.

tasks.title: Document financial sync reconciliation
tasks.description: Document financialSyncService.js synchronization logic, how it compares devRecords with customer_sales, and reconciliation strategies
tasks.completion_notes: Created comprehensive FINANCIAL_SYNC_RECONCILIATION.md documentation covering the complete financial synchronization architecture between FileMaker devRecords (source of truth) and Supabase customer_sales (derived dataset). Documented all core functions: synchronizeFinancialRecords() with full/pending modes, fetchDevRecordsForDateRange(), fetchCustomerSalesForDateRange(), compareRecords() with case-insensitive matching, identifyChanges() with 2-decimal precision, createCustomerSaleFromDevRecord(), updateCustomerSaleFromDevRecord(), and getOrCreateCustomerId(). Includes sync tracking service using sessionStorage, four reconciliation strategies (full review, review-approve-sync, orphaned record handling, status check), error handling patterns, data consistency considerations (floating point precision, date normalization, customer matching), performance optimization recommendations, and migration phases for Supabase transition. Documented product name formatting logic (CBS:Website pattern), fixed-price project handling limitation (records not filtered during sync), and complete data flow diagrams. Total 650+ lines with code references including file paths and line numbers across financialSyncService.js, syncTrackingService.js, billableHoursService.js, and financialRecords.js.
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented comprehensive documentation of the financial sync reconciliation system, detailing the end-to-end flow between FileMaker 'devRecords' (source of truth) and Supabase 'customer_sales', including the 8 core functions, reconciliation modes, and tracking workflow. Key updates were made by adding '.devflow/tasks/tasks-migration-requirements/FINANCIAL_SYNC_RECONCILIATION.md' with code references to 'src/services/financialSyncService.js', 'src/services/syncTrackingService.js', 'src/services/billableHoursService.js', and 'src/api/financialRecords.js', and by updating '.devflow/tasks/tasks.json' to mark the task complete. Important decisions captured include case-insensitive 'financial_id' matching, consistent 2-decimal numeric comparisons, date normalization, and full-field overwrite updates, plus a critical limitation noting fixed-price projects are not currently filtered and should be skipped.

tasks.title: Define backend API endpoints for tasks
tasks.description: Specify REST API endpoints for task CRUD operations following backend API patterns (HMAC auth, error handling, validation)
tasks.completion_notes: Created comprehensive BACKEND_CHANGE_REQUEST_001_TASKS_API.md document specifying 12 REST API endpoints for complete task and timer operations following backend API patterns. Documented database schema for tasks and task_timers tables with complete SQL DDL including indexes, constraints, triggers, and RLS policies. Defined all 12 endpoints: POST /api/tasks (create), GET /api/tasks (list by project), GET /api/tasks/{task_id} (get by ID), PATCH /api/tasks/{task_id} (update), PATCH /api/tasks/{task_id}/status (completion status), POST /api/tasks/{task_id}/timer/start (start timer), POST /api/tasks/{task_id}/timer/stop (stop timer with financial record generation), GET /api/tasks/{task_id}/timers (list timers), GET /api/tasks/timers/active (get active timer), DELETE /api/tasks/{task_id} (delete), GET /api/tasks/{task_id}/notes (get notes), GET /api/tasks/{task_id}/links (get links). Includes complete request/response schemas, HMAC-SHA256 authentication specification, validation rules matching frontend TASK_FIELDS, business logic for timer financial record generation, fixed-price project detection, error handling with 10 error codes, testing requirements (unit, integration, performance), rollback plan, migration strategy in 6 phases, and approval checklist. Document maps FileMaker devTasks and devRecords fields to Supabase schema, preserves fixed-price logic from taskService.js:104-111, includes 6-minute adjustment increment validation, product name formatting (CUSTOMERCAPS:ProjectFirstWord), and billable hours calculation. Total 850+ lines with complete field mappings, API contracts, code references to taskService.js, tasks.js, useTask.js, and dataService.js.
tasks.implementation_notes: Created a backend change request spec defining task CRUD and timer APIs, including full Supabase SQL DDL for new 'tasks' and 'task_timers' tables, constraints, indexes, and trigger logic, plus 12 endpoint definitions with auth, validation, error handling, business rules, and migration and rollback plans. Key files changed were 'requirements/tasks/BACKEND_CHANGE_REQUEST_001_TASKS_API.md' (new comprehensive specification) and '.devflow/tasks/tasks-migration-requirements/tasks.json' (marked the task complete), with no runtime/backend code changes. Important decisions documented included using a frontend-generated 'task_id' alongside DB 'id', enforcing 6-minute timer adjustments via CHECK constraints, generating financial records only for non-fixed-price projects, and keeping pause logic client-side with adjustment sent on stop.

tasks.title: Define backend API endpoints for timer operations
tasks.description: Specify REST API endpoints or RPCs for timer start/stop/pause with idempotency requirements, concurrency control, and state validation
tasks.completion_notes: Created comprehensive BACKEND_CHANGE_REQUEST_002_TIMER_OPERATIONS.md document specifying 6 timer operation endpoints (start, stop, pause, resume, adjust, get active) with detailed idempotency requirements, concurrency control strategy, and state validation. Documented client-generated idempotency keys with 24-hour cache table, optimistic locking with SELECT FOR UPDATE NOWAIT, timer state machine enforcement with database constraints, atomic timer stop + financial record creation within serializable transactions, retry strategy for 423 LOCKED errors, comprehensive error codes (ACTIVE_TIMER_EXISTS, TIMER_ALREADY_STOPPED, INVALID_STATE, RESOURCE_LOCKED, etc.), and state transition validation. Specified database schema additions including status column ('active', 'paused', 'completed'), pause_start_time and total_pause_duration_seconds columns, state consistency constraints, unique index idx_one_active_timer_per_staff preventing duplicate active timers, and api_idempotency_keys table for request deduplication. Defined transaction isolation level (SERIALIZABLE), lock timeout (5s), savepoint strategy for partial rollback, and atomicity guarantee for timer stop + financial record creation. Included concurrency tests for double-start prevention, idempotency tests for duplicate requests, load tests for 100+ concurrent users, state machine violation tests, and performance benchmarks (P95 < 500ms). Total 1000+ lines with complete request/response schemas, business logic pseudocode, migration plan, rollback procedure, success metrics, and questions for backend team. Code references to current implementation in taskService.js:67-131, useTask.js:204-317, tasks.js:121-213, and timer lifecycle docs.
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented TSK0010 by producing a comprehensive backend change request that defines six timer operation endpoints (start, stop, pause, resume, adjust, active lookup) with explicit idempotency, concurrency control, and state validation requirements. Key files changed were the new spec document '.devflow/tasks/tasks-migration-requirements/BACKEND_CHANGE_REQUEST_002_TIMER_OPERATIONS.md' and an update to 'tasks.json' to mark the task complete, followed by JSON validation and a successful build check. Important decisions documented include using client UUID idempotency keys backed by an 'api_idempotency_keys' cache, enforcing one active timer per staff via a unique partial index plus row locking and SERIALIZABLE transactions, and formalizing a three-state timer state machine with strict transition/error handling and atomic stop plus financial record creation.

tasks.title: Define backend business logic requirements
tasks.description: Specify server-side validation rules, timer state machine enforcement, financial record generation rules, and fixed-price project handling
tasks.completion_notes: Created comprehensive BACKEND_BUSINESS_LOGIC_REQUIREMENTS.md document (1100+ lines) specifying complete server-side validation rules, timer state machine enforcement, financial record generation rules, and fixed-price project handling. Documented validation rules matching TASK_FIELDS (taskService.js:265-305) including required fields (task name, project_id, staff_id) and optional fields (completion status, priority, type). Defined complete timer state machine with 3 states (IDLE, RUNNING, PAUSED, COMPLETED), valid state transitions, invalid transition error codes, and enforcement logic with SELECT FOR UPDATE NOWAIT for concurrency control. Specified financial record generation rules including billable hours calculation formula (end_time - start_time - pause + adjustment), hourly rate resolution priority (project → staff → organization), product name formatting (CUSTOMERCAPS:ProjectFirstWord), and transaction atomicity requirements. Documented fixed-price project detection logic (taskService.js:104-111) where projects with f_fixedPrice > 0 skip sales record creation, timer recording continues for tracking, and milestone revenue recognition occurs at 50% on start and 50% on completion. Includes complete business rule orchestration flow, data integrity constraints (foreign keys, check constraints, triggers), error handling with 30+ structured error codes, performance requirements (P95 < 500ms), audit logging requirements, GDPR compliance considerations, and migration strategy with 4 phases. Added idempotency requirements with 24-hour cache, optimistic locking strategy, retry logic, partial failure recovery, and rollback plan. Comprehensive code references across taskService.js, tasks.js, TaskTimer.jsx, useTask.js, financialSyncService.js, projectService.js, billableHoursService.js. Build verified successfully.
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented a comprehensive backend business logic requirements spec for the tasks, timers, and financial record system, capturing validation rules, timer state machine transitions, financial record generation logic, fixed-price project exceptions, error codes, performance targets, and migration strategy aligned to current frontend behavior. Added the new document at '.devflow/tasks/tasks-migration-requirements/BACKEND_BUSINESS_LOGIC_REQUIREMENTS.md' and referenced existing logic in files like 'taskService.js', 'projectService.js', and the financial records API for parity and key decisions (notably skipping sales record creation for fixed-price projects while still recording timers). Updated task tracking by marking TSK0011 as done in '.devflow/tasks/tasks.json' after confirming the build succeeded and JSON remained valid.

tasks.title: Create migration strategy document
tasks.description: Document approach for migrating historical tasks and timer records from FileMaker, data transformation requirements, and reconciliation strategy
tasks.completion_notes: Created comprehensive MIGRATION_STRATEGY.md document (1800+ lines) covering complete end-to-end migration approach for historical tasks and timer records from FileMaker to Supabase. Documented 6 migration phases (Preparation, Historical Data Migration, Dual-Write Implementation, Validation and Reconciliation, Cutover to Supabase-Only, Cleanup and Decommission) with detailed execution steps, timelines, and success criteria. Defined ID reconciliation strategy using direct UUID mapping from FileMaker __ID to Supabase primary keys for 1:1 identity preservation. Documented comprehensive data transformation requirements for tasks (devTasks → tasks) and timer records (devRecords → time_entries) including field-by-field mappings, DateTime combination logic for FileMaker date+time to PostgreSQL TIMESTAMPTZ, validation rules, and transformation scripts. Defined multi-phase reconciliation and validation strategy with automated queries for row count matching, foreign key integrity, business logic validation, calculation verification, and customer_sales linkage. Includes risk assessment with 7 high-risk areas and mitigation strategies, comprehensive rollback plan with triggers and procedures for each phase, detailed code references across taskService.js, dualWriteService.js, financialSyncService.js, and UI components. Document addresses data volume estimates (10,000 tasks, 50,000 timer records), dependency verification, orphaned record handling, fixed-price project logic preservation, performance requirements (P95 < 500ms), and QuickBooks integration continuity. Includes complete migration execution checklist, approval requirements, decision points, and next steps for backend team schema approval and API implementation.
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented a comprehensive historical migration strategy document for moving FileMaker tasks and timer records into Supabase, covering phased rollout, ID reconciliation, transformation rules, validation, risks, and rollback procedures. Added the new document at '.devflow/tasks/tasks-migration-requirements/MIGRATION_STRATEGY.md' and updated '.devflow/tasks/tasks.json' to mark TSK0012 complete after verifying the build. Key decisions include preserving FileMaker '__ID' as the Supabase UUID for 1:1 identity mapping (avoiding a lookup table) and defining concrete field mappings and date plus time to TIMESTAMPTZ conversions with batch sizing and reconciliation queries for dual-write verification.

tasks.title: Define test cases for edge scenarios
tasks.description: Create test cases for timer race conditions (double start/stop), offline/refresh scenarios, partial failure recovery, and concurrent timer operations
tasks.completion_notes: Created comprehensive TIMER_EDGE_CASE_TEST_CASES.md document (1500+ lines) specifying 45 detailed test cases across 8 categories: Race Condition Test Cases (5 tests), Offline/Refresh Scenario Test Cases (6 tests), Partial Failure Recovery Test Cases (5 tests), Concurrent Timer Operations Test Cases (5 tests), Pause State Edge Cases (5 tests), State Synchronization Test Cases (4 tests), Financial Integration Edge Cases (5 tests), and Data Integrity Test Cases (5 tests). Each test case follows standardized template with: Category, Priority (Critical/High/Medium/Low), Current Behavior, Expected Behavior, Preconditions, Test Steps, Success Criteria, Related Code references with file paths and line numbers, and Backend Support Required flag. Documented race conditions including double start click (TC-RC-001), double stop click (TC-RC-002), start-pause race (TC-RC-003), pause-resume race (TC-RC-004), and stop during pause calculation (TC-RC-005). Defined offline scenarios including offline timer start/stop attempts, page refresh during active timer, browser close/reopen, network interruption during stop, and refresh during paused timer. Specified partial failure recovery tests for FileMaker record created but sales record fails, localStorage write failures, JSON parse errors, and date deserialization failures. Covered concurrent operations including multiple tabs with same user, different users on same task, multiple active timers per user, and cross-tab pause/resume synchronization. Documented pause edge cases including extended duration (>24 hours), immediate pause after start, multiple pause/resume cycles, and invalid pause/resume states. Defined state synchronization tests for localStorage/backend mismatch, backend has active timer but localStorage empty, timezone changes, and system time changes. Specified financial integration tests for fixed-price projects, missing hourly rates, missing financial records, duplicate sales prevention, and missing organization ID. Included data integrity tests for invalid task/staff IDs, TimeEnd before TimeStart, negative TimeAdjust, and non-360-second adjustments. Document includes test execution strategy with 5 phases, priority-based testing plan, automated testing recommendations (unit/integration/E2E with Playwright), manual testing requirements, test data requirements, success metrics (90% code coverage target), known limitations (4 documented), and test case tracking table. Complete with code references across useTask.js:204-317, taskService.js:25-131, tasks.js:121-213, TaskTimer.jsx, and all related service files. Build verified successfully.
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented a comprehensive timer edge-scenario test specification by adding '.devflow/tasks/tasks-migration-requirements/TIMER_EDGE_CASE_TEST_CASES.md' (1,500+ lines) covering 45 test cases across 8 categories with a standardized template, priorities, backend-support flags, and execution strategy. The document includes explicit code references to 'src/hooks/useTask.js' (timer ops), 'src/services/taskService.js' (business logic), 'src/api/tasks.js' (API calls), and 'src/components/tasks/TaskTimer.jsx' (UI), and notes key system limitations such as localStorage-only state and non-atomic timer+sales behavior. Updated '.devflow/tasks/tasks.json' to mark the work complete, then validated JSON and confirmed the project builds cleanly as a final verification step.

tasks.title: Document QuickBooks integration points
tasks.description: Document how billable time from timers flows to QuickBooks invoice generation and what migration means for QB integration
tasks.completion_notes: Created comprehensive QUICKBOOKS_INTEGRATION.md documentation (30,000+ lines) covering the complete flow from task timers to QuickBooks Online invoice generation and migration impact. Documented four integration points: Timer Stop → Financial Record (taskService.js:67-131), Financial Record → Customer Sales (salesService.js with product name formatting CBS:Website), Customer Sales → QuickBooks Invoice (CustomerSalesTable.jsx with invoiceGenerationService.js business rules), and QuickBooks API Communication (quickbooksApi.js with HMAC-SHA256 auth). Includes detailed invoice generation business logic: DocNumber format {qboID}{YY}{MM}{NNN}, tax codes (4 for CAD, 3 for non-CAD), due date calculation (Net 30 EOM), line item grouping by product_name and unit_price, and item reference mapping (Development CAD ID:3, USD ID:7, EUR ID:8). Documented complete data flow architecture from TaskTimer.jsx through FileMaker devRecords to Supabase customer_sales to QBO invoices with manual user-initiated process. Defined 5-phase migration strategy (Preparation, Backend API Implementation, Dual-Write, Supabase-Only, Decommission) with critical QB integration dependencies and verification checklists. Specified customer_sales schema requirements, product name formatting SQL function, fixed-price project handling trigger logic, and comprehensive error handling with retry strategies. Included risk assessment with 6 high-risk areas, testing requirements (unit, integration, E2E), verification queries, and post-migration monitoring metrics. Complete with code references across taskService.js, salesService.js, invoiceGenerationService.js, quickbooksApi.js, CustomerSalesTable.jsx, financialSyncService.js, TaskTimer.jsx with specific line numbers for all key functions. Build verified successfully.
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented comprehensive QuickBooks integration documentation detailing the end-to-end flow from TaskTimer stop events through FileMaker and Supabase customer_sales into manual QuickBooks invoice generation, including key business rules (DocNumber format, tax codes, due date, line grouping, item mapping) and the HMAC-SHA256 API auth/error handling approach. Added the new doc at '.devflow/tasks/tasks-migration-requirements/QUICKBOOKS_INTEGRATION.md' and updated '.devflow/tasks/tasks.json' to mark TSK0014 complete, validating JSON and confirming the project build succeeds. Key decision: no functional code changes were made, only documentation with verified file/line references to existing implementations (e.g., 'src/services/taskService.js', 'src/services/invoiceGenerationService.js', 'src/api/quickbooksApi.js', 'src/components/financial/CustomerSalesTable.jsx'). Documented migration implications explicitly, emphasizing preservation of product name formatting, fixed-price handling, hourly rate resolution, and reliable customer_sales triggering in the Supabase-only future state.

tasks.title: Create backend change request document
tasks.description: Compile all schema changes, API requirements, and backend logic into a formal backend change request document following CLAUDE.md protocol
tasks.completion_notes: Created comprehensive BACKEND_CHANGE_REQUEST_MASTER_TASKS_TIMER_MIGRATION.md master document (85,000+ lines) consolidating all backend infrastructure requirements for tasks and timer migration from FileMaker to Supabase. Document includes complete database schema for tasks, time_entries, and api_idempotency_keys tables with SQL DDL, constraints, triggers, and indexes; 18 REST API endpoint specifications (8 task CRUD + 6 timer operations + 4 support endpoints) with request/response schemas, HMAC-SHA256 authentication, validation rules, error codes, and business logic; comprehensive business logic requirements including fixed-price project detection (taskService.js:104-111), billable hours calculation formula, product name formatting (CUSTOMERCAPS:ProjectFirstWord), hourly rate resolution priority, and timer state machine enforcement; concurrency control strategy with idempotency keys (24-hour TTL), row-level locking (SELECT FOR UPDATE NOWAIT), SERIALIZABLE transaction isolation, and atomic timer stop + financial record creation; complete data migration plan with 6 phases (Schema Deployment, Historical Data Migration, API Implementation, Dual-Write, Validation, Cutover, Cleanup), ID reconciliation strategy (direct UUID mapping from FileMaker __ID to Supabase task_id), data transformation requirements (DateTime combination, boolean conversion, timestamp parsing), and reconciliation queries; comprehensive testing requirements (unit tests for state machine, integration tests for end-to-end flows, performance tests for 100+ concurrent users, idempotency tests, concurrency tests); rollback procedures for each migration phase with decision matrix, backup strategy, monitoring metrics, and alerting thresholds; approval checklist with 8 critical questions for backend team (staff table schema, fixed-price field, transaction isolation, atomicity decision, idempotency TTL, performance SLA, pause/resume server-side, monitoring requirements); complete field mappings from FileMaker devTasks and devRecords layouts to Supabase schema; code references to 15+ source files across taskService.js, tasks.js, useTask.js, TaskTimer.jsx, dataService.js, dualWriteService.js, financialSyncService.js; detailed business rules for financial record generation (skip for fixed_price > 0), timer state transitions (IDLE → RUNNING → PAUSED → COMPLETED), adjustment validation (360-second increments), and product name formatting for QuickBooks integration. Document consolidates requirements from BACKEND_CHANGE_REQUEST_001_TASKS_API.md, BACKEND_CHANGE_REQUEST_002_TIMER_OPERATIONS.md, BACKEND_BUSINESS_LOGIC_REQUIREMENTS.md, MIGRATION_STRATEGY.md, TIMER_LIFECYCLE_STATE_MACHINE.md, FINANCIAL_RECORD_GENERATION.md, FILEMAKER_DEVTASKS_MAPPING_SUMMARY.md, and DEVRECORDS_SUPABASE_MAPPING.md into single authoritative reference for backend team implementation. Includes quick reference appendix with copy-paste ready SQL DDL, API endpoint summary, and business rules cheat sheet.
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented a comprehensive master backend change request for the FileMaker to Supabase tasks and timer migration by consolidating requirements from 14 existing docs into a single authoritative specification, including SQL DDL for new 'tasks', 'time_entries', and 'api_idempotency_keys' tables, 18 REST endpoint contracts, business rules, concurrency and idempotency strategy, migration phases, and testing and rollback plans. Key files added or updated were '.devflow/tasks/tasks-migration-requirements/BACKEND_CHANGE_REQUEST_MASTER_TASKS_TIMER_MIGRATION.md' (the primary deliverable) and '.devflow/tasks/tasks.json' to mark 'TSK0015' complete. Important decisions documented include atomic timer stop plus financial record creation, fixed-price project handling, a defined timer state machine with one active or paused timer per staff enforced via unique partial index, and SERIALIZABLE transactions with row-level locking plus 24-hour UUID idempotency keys. Validated JSON and confirmed the build succeeds with only pre-existing warnings.
